message:
feat(extensions): budget-based structured concurrency + OAuth config extraction

Add per-operation timeout budgets to the extension runtime, replacing
unbounded Cx::for_request() calls with deadline-constrained contexts.
Also extract OAuth configuration from extension provider manifests.

Budget constants (milliseconds):
- EXTENSION_TOOL_BUDGET_MS: 30,000
- EXTENSION_COMMAND_BUDGET_MS: 30,000
- EXTENSION_SHORTCUT_BUDGET_MS: 30,000
- EXTENSION_UI_BUDGET_MS: 1,000
- EXTENSION_PROVIDER_BUDGET_MS: 120,000
- EXTENSION_QUERY_BUDGET_MS: 10,000
- EXTENSION_LOAD_BUDGET_MS: 60,000

Key changes:
- cx_with_deadline() helper: creates a Cx with Budget containing a
  wall-clock deadline, used throughout JsExtensionRuntimeHandle methods
- All runtime handle methods (load_extension, call_tool, execute_command,
  execute_shortcut, deliver_event, stream_message, query) now use
  budget-constrained contexts instead of unbounded ones
- OAuth config extraction from extension manifests: parses
  provider_spec.oauth object to build OAuthConfig (authUrl, tokenUrl,
  clientId, scopes, redirectUri) and attaches it to ModelEntry
- New lifecycle/structured concurrency tests for runtime shutdown,
  ExtensionRegion, custom budgets, weak-ref cycle breaking

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
